channel.title: POLITICO Pulse
channel.description: Delivered daily by 10 a.m., Pulse examines the latest news in health care politics and policy.

item.title: Blue states cut ties with RFK Jr.’s vaccine panel
item.description: Maryland became the latest in a procession of blue states moving to decouple their laws from a federal vaccine advisory panel overhauled by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Kennedy’s complex history
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., who has called McDonald’s food “poison,” once handed out McDonald’s hamburgers and chicken nuggets to firefighters digging through the rubble at Ground Zero in the days after the 9/11 attacks.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: MAHA approval ratings
item.description: New results from The POLITICO Poll suggest that despite the controversies Kennedy and his acolytes have been embroiled in, the public isn’t too concerned.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper

item.title: White House extends olive branch to MAHA
item.description: The White House is mending fences with some of the loudest supporters of Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Make America Healthy Again movement.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: RFK Jr. maps out a new vaccine panel strategy
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s handpicked slate of vaccine advisers was frozen by a federal judge last month, who said many of the members lacked the qualifications to make recommendations on immunizations.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: The revived Medicare generics push
item.description: Bipartisan lawmakers have revived efforts to require Medicare to cover generic drugs.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Private Medicare’s payday
item.description: Medicare Advantage insurers will see their federal payments increase by about $13 billion next year — a win for the private plans after the Trump administration had initially proposed nearly flat funding for 2027.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: The MAHA identity crisis
item.description: There’s disagreement within MAHA over which policies align with the movement.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Trump’s looming budget proposal and HHS’s future
item.description: The Trump administration is expected to send its fiscal 2027 budget request to Congress today — offering a key insight into how it views the future of HHS and its subagencies.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Drug-pricing middlemen push back on reforms
item.description: In recent years in Washington, pharmacy benefit managers have been under attack from all sides.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Foreign doctors’ visa frenzy
item.description: Thousands of doctors from countries on the Trump administration’s travel-ban list are stuck in visa limbo.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Senator vows to take on RFK Jr. and ‘polarized’ HHS
item.description: Democratic Sen. Angela Alsobrooks of Maryland said her goal is to expose the danger of Kennedy’s tenure as she sees it and mitigate the damage.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: The health care fight in the Tar Heel State
item.description: Both Republicans and Democrats look to capitalize on President Donald Trump’s and the Congressional GOP’s changes to health policy.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: MAHA fans want action Trump hasn’t delivered
item.description: The Make America Healthy Again movement joined forces with Republicans in 2024, helping hand Donald Trump the presidency. The midterms might look different.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: CDC director pick in limbo
item.description: The White House is holding off on picking a permanent CDC director as the acting director’s time leading the agency expires today.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Medicaid's AI ‘cultural shift’
item.description: CMS Administrator Mehmet Oz wants to change the culture around artificial intelligence at his agency.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Who runs MAHA? Most Americans draw a blank
item.description: Under the second Trump administration, federal health agencies have been thrust into the national spotlight amid unprecedented layoffs and policy overhauls in the name of the Make America Healthy Again movement.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Supercharging the peptide boom
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said last month that a key front in what he calls the FDA’s “war on public health” was nearing its end.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Democrats pitch one-stop health plans
item.description: Senate Democrats unveiled a list of health policy goals Thursday aimed at lowering costs and reining in private insurance companies.
item.dc:creator: By Simon J. Levien, Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Simon J. Levien, Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Obamacare Pulse check
item.description: Congress let enhanced Obamacare subsidies lapse at the end of 2025, and early indicators are pointing to major shifts in the Affordable Care Act marketplace this year.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Lawmakers’ health care fraud clash
item.description: On Tuesday, Republicans and Democrats on a key House committee sparred over GOP efforts to investigate Medicaid fraud.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Can Bhattacharya take the heat?
item.description: Jay Bhattacharya, NIH director and newly minted acting CDC head, has so far avoided much of the harsh criticism levied at Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s HHS.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: The celebrity doctor with Epstein ties
item.description: Previously unreported records suggest Peter Attia had a deeper relationship with Epstein than he publicly acknowledged last month.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: The Medicare cost conundrum
item.description: An independent congressional agency estimates the federal government will overpay Medicare Advantage by nearly $80 billion in 2026 compared with traditional Medicare — or about 14 percent more.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: MAHA courts women voters at HHS health event
item.description: What do an Alzheimer’s researcher and Miss America’s Teen 2026 have in common?
item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun, Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun, Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Biotech CEOs sound alarm on Trump drug-pricing plan
item.description: As Congress mulls whether to follow President Donald Trump’s orders for a most-favored-nation drug-pricing policy, biotech firm executives are starting to speak out against the measure.
item.dc:creator: By Simon J. Levien, Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Simon J. Levien, Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Clock ticks on CDC pick
item.description: The Trump administration is running out of time to select a nominee to run the CDC if it wants NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya, who’s also serving as the CDC’s acting director, to stay in dual roles.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Democrats’ quest to take back MAHA
item.description: The Trump administration’s promotion of glyphosate, a pesticide used in industrial farming, has driven a wedge into the Make America Healthy Again coalition.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Casey’s vaccine dilemma
item.description: Casey Means, President Donald Trump’s pick for surgeon general, is too pro-vaccine for some of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s base, but too anti-vaccine for the medical establishment.
item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Kelly Hooper

item.title: The Trump policy both parties agree on
item.description: An increasing number of states are using a Trump-backed policy to encourage businesses to help their workers sign up for Obamacare.
item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Kelly Hooper and Sophie Gardner
